assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a machine that can install Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x and npm.
No Atlassian account, Forge API token, cloud developer site, or Forge app exists yet.
No Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, or Jira Service Management host product has been preselected.

developer_goal: Create an Atlassian developer identity and reach the first visible Forge hello-world app success on a chosen Atlassian cloud host product.

first_success.outcome: For a selected host-product hello-world route such as Jira: the Forge app panel is visible on the host surface with the configured title and hello-world content.
first_success.observable_signal: On the Jira candidate, the app panel is visible on a Jira issue after Apps selection; other host products have different navigation and completion signals.

route_selection.surface: Atlassian Forge CLI and free cloud developer site
route_selection.rule: Complete shared Forge setup, then select one host-product hello-world tutorial. Do not invent a winner among Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, and Jira Service Management when the shared guide presents them as peers.

candidates.name: Forge hello world for Jira
candidates.reason: Fully documented after shared setup, but the shared Getting started guide does not prefer Jira over the other host products.

candidates.name: Forge hello world for Confluence
candidates.reason: Peer official tutorial with a different module, install context, and visible success surface.

candidates.name: Forge hello world for Bitbucket or Jira Service Management
candidates.reason: Peer official tutorials; choosing either changes entitlement, template, and verification navigation.

candidates.name: Compass or non-Forge integration path
candidates.reason: Different developer surface from the Forge getting-started entry the corpus selected for discovery.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to an Atlassian account used for Forge development.

required_fields.label: Atlassian account identity
required_fields.notes: Cloud developer signup offers email and social identity options; exact required fields for a cold email signup are not fully inventoried in Forge docs.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an Atlassian account email usable for API token and cloud developer site creation.

required_path.action: Install supported Node.js LTS and the Forge CLI, then verify both with version commands.
required_path.observable_result: node --version and forge --version report supported versions.

required_path.action: Create a scoped Forge API token and run forge login with the account email and token.
required_path.observable_result: Forge CLI reports a successful login.

required_path.action: Create the free Atlassian cloud developer site and complete its setup wizard.
required_path.observable_result: A free cloud developer site with Confluence and Jira applications is ready.

required_path.action: Choose one host product, create the hello-world app from its template, deploy, and install onto the developer site.

required_fields.label: Host product
required_fields.notes: Product choice is required and unresolved across peer tutorials.

required_path.observable_result: forge deploy and forge install complete for the selected site.

required_path.action: Open the host-product surface required by the selected tutorial and confirm the hello-world UI is visible.
required_path.observable_result: The configured hello-world content is visible on the selected host product.

prerequisites.description: Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x is required before installing the Forge CLI.
Forge apps cannot be viewed by anonymous users; the tester must be logged in to the cloud developer site.

external_gates.description: A scoped API token with Forge selected must be created in the Atlassian account security settings before CLI login.
forge install requires authorizing the app onto the chosen Atlassian site context.

unavoidable_waits.description: Atlassian must finish provisioning the free cloud developer site before install and verification.

platform_outcomes.description: Forge CLI stores authenticated developer credentials after forge login.
forge deploy builds and deploys the app; forge install links it to the developer site.

excluded.item: Reading Forge conceptual documentation beyond Getting started and the selected hello-world tutorial
excluded.reason: Reading is not a measured product action.
Occurs after local install and hello-world verification.
Different surface from the shared Forge setup entry.

sources.title: Prepare to build your first Forge app
Get started integrating with Compass
evidence_supported: Compass as an alternate integration surface

sources.title: Atlassian cloud developer site signup
evidence_supported: Free development sites for Jira Software, Jira Service Management, and Confluence

sources.title: Build a Jira hello world app

uncertainties.question: Which Atlassian host product should define the first visible Forge app: Bitbucket, Confluence, Jira, or Jira Service Management?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes template, manifest module, install prompts, navigation, and completion signal, so lexicographic selection cannot pick a winner among peer official tutorials.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded product goal that names one host app, then confirmation that its hello-world route is the intended organization-wide first mile.

uncertainties.question: What exact required fields appear on Atlassian account and cloud developer site signup for a cold email identity?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation field counts remain unverified before shared Forge setup.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of Atlassian account creation and go.atlassian.com/cloud-dev without pre-existing login.
